caption: Hanuman offers Rama's ring to Sita in the Ashoka grove
scene: By night in the Ashoka grove of Lanka, the humble Hanuman kneels and offers Lord Rama's golden signet ring to Sita as a sign that he comes from Rama; Sita, seated beneath the Ashoka tree, lifts her hand to her heart in sudden hope. The rakshasi guards sleep nearby; golden Lanka glows across the water under the full moon. Rama is not present — Hanuman is his messenger.
prompt: Traditional Tanjore (Thanjavur) style devotional painting, wide landscape 16:9: in the Ashoka grove of Lanka by night, the humble Hanuman (a noble vanara) kneeling on one knee, head bowed, extending one hand palm-up to offer Lord Rama's golden signet ring to Sita as a sign that he comes from Rama. Sita sits beneath the flowering Ashoka tree, her face lit with hope; a few rakshasi guards sleep nearby; golden Lanka glows across the water under a full moon. NO Rama in the scene (he is far away). Rich jewel-tone colors, gold-leaf gilded ornamentation and halos, ornate arched temple border, serene rounded figures, classical South Indian temple art, no text, no lettering.
